text.title: AWS Monitoring + Observability Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules every application of `AWS Monitoring + Observability` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Production alarms MUST be derived from SLOs (availability, latency, error rate) using multi-window burn-rate (5min + 1h windows minimum). Static thresholds without SLO grounding are forbidden.

rationale.source: Google SRE Workbook ch. 5; AWS Alarms Best Practices

rule.rationale: Threshold-based alarms either over-page (noise) or under-page (miss real degradation); SLO-burn alarms are mathematically grounded.

rule.statement: Application logs MUST be JSON-structured with request_id + service + trace_id; CloudWatch Logs Insights queries MUST be saved per service.
rule.rationale: Free-text logs cannot be queried at scale; JSON logs power Insights dashboards + alarms.

rule.statement: Inter-service calls MUST emit X-Ray traces; sampling rate ≥ 5% for prod, 100% for critical paths.
rule.rationale: Without distributed tracing, debugging cross-service latency is impossible; X-Ray + OTLP gives the dependency map.

rule.statement: Custom CloudWatch metrics MUST live under a per-service namespace `<Team>/<Service>`; emitting to `AWS/*` namespaces is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Polluting AWS namespaces hides custom metrics; per-service namespace makes ownership + cost attribution clean.

rule.statement: Each production service MUST have a CloudWatch / Grafana dashboard with: SLI gauges (availability, p50/p95/p99 latency, error rate), saturation panels (CPU / memory / connection pool), and a deploy-marker overlay.

rationale.source: Google SRE Workbook; AWS dashboard examples

rule.rationale: No dashboard = blind during incident; standard dashboard accelerates triage.

rule.statement: Log retention MUST be tiered: 30d hot in CloudWatch, 90-365d in S3 (subscription filter), > 1y in Glacier. Indefinite retention is flagged as cost finding.
rule.rationale: Default infinite retention dominates CloudWatch Logs cost; tiered retention is 80%+ cheaper.

rule.statement: ECS / EKS clusters MUST have Container Insights enabled; node + pod-level metrics MUST flow into a unified dashboard.
rule.rationale: Without Container Insights, pod-level investigations require shell access; metrics-first model is auditable.

rule.statement: New services MUST emit OpenTelemetry (OTLP) traces + metrics; AWS Distro for OpenTelemetry collector deployed per cluster / per VPC.
rule.rationale: Vendor-locking observability to a single SaaS is expensive long-term; OTel preserves portability.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
